Running PICO Executables

PICO provides a general mechanism for specifying run-time parameters. The parameters in the PICO Core are defined in the picoParams and parPicoParams classes. User applications can add an unlimited number of their own run-time parameters, so long as their names are different from these parameters (see documentation for the UTILIB ParameterSet class).

Although PICO was principally designed for use as a library, several executables that are built with PIC0 to illustrate the use of PICO within various applications: knapsack, lipshitzian and PICO. Section Using the PICO LP and MILP Solver describes the use of the PICO solver to solve mixed-integer linear programming problems. The command-line syntax for running these solvers with optional command-line parameter is 

  PICO --param1 <paramval1> --param2=<paramval2> <data-file1> <data-file2> ...
PICO solvers support the use of the GNU style parameter keywords that are preceded by two hyphens rather than keyword letters. A parameter argument (if any) can be separated by either whitespace or a single equal sign character. If not parameter value is include, a default value of 'true' is assumed.

PICO supports online documentation of solver parameters, which can be accessed with the '--help' parameter option: 

  PICO --help
The commandline option prints out general descriptive information for all options that are supported by a PICO solver. The remainder of this section describes key parameters, and their usage model within PICO.

Debugging parameters

The following parameters support debugging within PICO solvers:
  • debug (default: 0)
    This integer parameter controls the general level of debugging information that is generated by PICO. The greater the number, the more data you get!

  • debug-solver-params (default: false)
    This boolean parameter can be used to print the value of all PICO parameters that are used. This is handy for debugging whether parameters were actually setup to the intended values, and for confirming the values of various control parameters that were not explicitly set.

  • earlyOutputMinutes (default: 0.0)
    If this real parameter is greater than zero, then after the specified number of sections have elapsed, the current incumbent will be written to the solution.dat file. This parameter is useful in contexts where PICO may crash, or be interrupted by a user.

  • statusPrintCount (default: 100000)
    Status printouts are used to summarize PICO's search progress. This parameter specifies how often status printouts are performed, as a function of the number of subproblems processed.

  • statusPrintSeconds (default: 10.0)
    Status printouts are used to summarize PICO's search progress. This parameter is used to ensure that status printouts are generated after the specified number of seconds.

  • use-abort (default: true or false)
    This parameter controls whether PICO will generate a core file when it aborts with an error. If PICO is compiled with debugging turned on, then this parameter is true by default. Otherwise, its default is false.

The --use-abort parameter is helpful for getting information about an unexpected error condition. This parameter forces the generation of a corefile that can be interrogated by a debugger like gdb. If PICO is compiled with debugging turned on, then debuggers can also be used with PICO solvers to interactively analyze the execution flow for errors. Note that PICO uses C++ exceptions, which can be difficult to trap. However, exceptions thrown by PICO are managed such that the function exit_fn is called before an exception is thrown. Thus, a user can set a breakpoint within this function to stop whenever an exception is thrown.
